perturbation.original_text: The parties agree that they will no t, during the course of their association with one another or, thereafter, disclose the information to any third party for any reason or purpose whatsoever without the prior written consent of the disclosing party, save in accordance with the provisions of this agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The parties agree that they will not disclose the information to any third party for monetary gain without the prior written consent of the disclosing party, save in accordance with the provisions of this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This change modifies the restriction on disclosing 'the information'. The original text prohibits disclosure for 'any reason or purpose whatsoever', while the change permits disclosure if it's not 'for monetary gain'. This introduces a loophole, as a party might disclose information for other reasons (e.g., strategic partnership) without written consent, creating an in-text contradiction with other sections expecting complete confidentiality.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The parties acknowledge that the information is a valuable, special and unique 3 asset proprietary to the disclosing party.
perturbation.justification: The original text prohibited disclosure of information for 'any reason or purpose whatsoever.' The changed text allows disclosure if it is not 'for monetary gain'. This creates an in-text contradiction because the 'Contradicted Text' states that 'the information is a valuable, special and unique asset proprietary to the disclosing party.' The changed text permits disclosure (for non-monetary reasons), directly conflicting with the expectation of complete confidentiality as the asset would be revealed regardless of the reason for disclosure. This is a strong contradiction as it weakens the protection of proprietary information. This is consistent with the definition of Ambiguities.

perturbation.original_text: The receiving party undertakes not to use the information fo r purpose other than: 5.2.1 that for which it is disclosed; and 5.2.2 in accordance with the provisions of this agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The receiving party undertakes not to use the information for purposes that directly compete with the disclosing party; use for other purposes is permissible, subject to applicable laws.
perturbation.explanation: The original text strictly limits the use of information to only the purpose it was disclosed. The changed text allows the receiving party to use the information for any purpose that doesn't directly compete with the disclosing party. This directly contradicts the original intent of restricting usage, as detailed in other clauses, by opening the door to using the information for purposes not originally agreed upon. This creates an internal contradiction and a significant ambiguity.
perturbation.contradicted_text: not to utilise, employ, exploit or in any other manner whatsoever use the information disclosed pursuant to the provisions of this agreem ent for any purpose without the prior express written consent of the disclosing party.
perturbation.justification: The original text restricts the receiving party's use of information strictly to the purpose for which it was disclosed. The changed text broadens this to allow any use that doesn't directly compete with the disclosing party, contradicting the original intent. The contradicted text explicitly prohibits using the information for any purpose without prior written consent. The changed text creates a clear conflict, as it permits uses beyond the disclosed purpose, which the contradicted text forbids, thus resulting in a strong in-text contradiction according to the 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: The parties agree that they shall protect the information disclosed pursuant to the provisions of this agreement using the s ame standard of care that each party applies to its own proprietary, secret or confidential information and that the information shall be stored and handled in such a way as to prevent any unauthorised disclosure thereof.
perturbation.changed_text: The parties agree that they shall protect the information disclosed pursuant to the provisions of this agreement using a reasonable standard of care.
perturbation.explanation: This modification introduces ambiguity by removing the specific benchmark of 'the same standard of care that each party applies to its own proprietary, secret or confidential information.' Replacing it with 'a reasonable standard of care' is vague and subjective. What constitutes 'reasonable' is open to interpretation, creating an in-text contradiction when combined with clauses that expect stringent protection equivalent to their own confidential data. It also impacts enforceability as the level of care is no longer tied to a measurable standard.
perturbation.contradicted_text: that the unauthorised disclosure of the information to a third party may cause irreparable loss, harm and damage to the disclosing party. Accordingly, the receiving par ty indemnifies and holds the disclosing party harmless against any loss, action, claim, harm or damage, of whatever nature, suffered by the disclosing party pursuant to a breach by the receiving party of the provisions of this agreement
perturbation.justification: The original text specified a standard of care tied to the party's own confidential information, creating a clear benchmark. The changed text replaces this with 'a reasonable standard of care,' which is subjective. This creates a contradiction because the contradicted text expects a breach to result in 'irreparable loss, harm and damage' and provides indemnification. A 'reasonable standard of care' may not be sufficient to prevent such damage, especially compared to the original expectation that the party would protect the information as they would their own confidential data. Therefore, the change introduces ambiguity and reduces the level of protection, contradicting the expectation of stringent protection and indemnification in the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: This agreement shall be deemed to have commenced upon the date of last 6 signing hereof and it shall continue to bind the parti es for 3 (three) years after its termination.
perturbation.changed_text: This agreement shall be deemed to have commenced upon the date of last 6 signing hereof and it shall continue to bind the parties for 3 (three) years, unless terminated earlier.
perturbation.explanation: This change adds 'unless terminated earlier' to the clause defining the agreement's term. While seemingly minor, it introduces uncertainty because the conditions for 'earlier termination' are not explicitly defined within this clause, contradicting the defined termination events in 9.2. The original text specified clear termination events, whereas this amendment opens the door to unspecified reasons for earlier termination, impacting the defined time that the parties shall be bind.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This agreement shall be deemed to have become terminated in the event of the following (whichever happens the sooner): 9.2.1 the discussions between the parties being unsuccessful and accordingly breaking down; or 9.2.2 an agreement between the parties being concluded regarding their future relationship; or 9.2.3 in any event not later than one year after signing hereof unless extended by mutual agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified that the agreement would bind the parties for three years after termination. The changed text introduces the phrase 'unless terminated earlier,' which contradicts the defined termination events in 9.2 (the contradicted text). While 9.2 specifies certain termination events, the added phrase opens the door to unspecified reasons for earlier termination, creating a conflict and uncertainty regarding the agreement's term and contradicting the terms outlined in 9.2. This ambiguity creates a strong in-text contradiction as it introduces a new condition for termination not aligned with the existing defined termination events.

perturbation.original_text: Any dispute arising out of or in connection with the provisions of this agreement shall be referred to arbitration and determined in accordance with the Arbitration Act 42 of 1965, as amended from time to time,
perturbation.changed_text: Any dispute arising out of or in connection with the provisions of this agreement may, at the discretion of the receiving party, be referred to arbitration and determined in accordance with the Arbitration Act 42 of 1965, as amended from time to time,
perturbation.explanation: This change alters the mandatory nature of arbitration. Originally, 'any dispute...shall be referred to arbitration.' The modified version states that disputes 'may, at the discretion of the receiving party, be referred to arbitration'. This introduces a significant in-text contradiction. The original text commits both parties to arbitration, but the change allows only the receiving party to decide if arbitration is used, contradicting the clauses stating arbitration as the solution for any dispute.
perturbation.contradicted_text: ENFOR CEMENT The failure to enforce or to require the performance at any time of any of the provisions of this agreement shall not be construed to be a waiver of such provision, and shall not affect either the validity of this agreement or any part hereof or th e right of any party to enforce the provisions of this agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates arbitration for any dispute. The changed text makes arbitration discretionary for the receiving party, contradicting the original intent and creating ambiguity about dispute resolution. This directly conflicts with the contradicted text, which emphasizes the enforceability of all provisions. If arbitration is no longer mandatory, the 'Enforcement' clause cannot guarantee the enforcement of arbitration as a dispute resolution method. Therefore, a strong contradiction exists.
